page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-96GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 16 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation model, meaning physical CPU resources are shared among tenants. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core and memory performance alongside weaker single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression is in the bottom 25%. However, multi-threaded operations, such as stress-ng multi-core, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core tests, consistently register in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, with read-only large block transfers reaching 48.36 GB/sec. LLM inference speeds for text generation on a medium 7B model are poor, though prompt processing for the same model remains average.

The shared CPU allocation model offers resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated single-core execution. The lack of bundled local storage requires external volume attachments. This server is suited for multi-threaded workloads that can tolerate shared CPU resources, such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and general-purpose application hosting.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-96GB is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 16 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 6.0 GB of memory per core but does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks place the server in the middle 50% tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and software compilation, though its single-core performance is limited, ranking in the bottom 10% on stress-ng tests. The shared CPU allocation model offers a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded workloads that do not demand continuous, dedicated CPU performance. It is suited for database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-96GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering average multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A shared-resource virtual server offering sixteen AMD EPYC cores and ninety-six gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads.

96.0 GB of system memory providing 6.0 GB per core
KVM hypervisor deployment with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Average multi-core performance in Geekbench and Passmark benchmarks

Suited for static web serving, database operations, and software compilation
